title: Add single-day availability override to Cal.com booking UI
feature_request: Users need a way to mark themselves as unavailable on a specific date, even if their regular schedule says they're available that day. Add this to the availability settings.
phase_1_expected_questions: Should the override apply to all event types or allow per-event-type overrides?
What UI should be used — a calendar date picker, a list of dates, or integration into the existing schedule view?
Should overrides support partial-day unavailability (e.g., unavailable 9am-12pm) or only full-day?
How should the override interact with existing bookings on that date — cancel them, keep them, or warn?

phase_1_answers.scope: Overrides apply globally across all event types for v1. Per-event-type overrides are a future enhancement.
phase_1_answers.ui: Add a date picker component to the existing availability settings page. Show overridden dates as a list below the regular schedule with the ability to remove them.
phase_1_answers.granularity: Full-day only for v1. The entire day is marked unavailable. Partial-day overrides add significant complexity and are deferred.
phase_1_answers.existing_bookings: Keep existing bookings intact. The override only affects future booking availability — slots on the overridden date no longer appear to new bookers. Existing bookings are honored.

phase_2_expected_plan_components: Database model or type for storing date overrides (date, userId, createdAt)
API endpoint or tRPC procedure for creating and deleting overrides
Integration with the availability checking logic to exclude overridden dates
Date picker UI component in the availability settings page
List view of active overrides with delete capability
Validation that override dates are in the future
phase_4_runtime_check: Run the TypeScript build for the UI package and verify it compiles without errors. Run the component tests and confirm the override component renders a date picker, displays overridden dates in a list, and correctly calls the delete handler when removing an override. Interpret the test output to confirm the feature integrates with the existing availability logic.
